mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 in subsequent quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer spending and residential investment, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model, with 20-day forward returns showing no significant positive skew during co-occurring uptrends in both series.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which signals near-term economic momentum and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during post-shock recovery periods, yielding negative 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered cases, or Rank IC drops below -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates expanding employment capacity and rising household income, which supports consumer demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns exhibit a negative correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 2-year rolling window, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below random chance with Rank IC below 0.0.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 195.0, it indicates accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten monetary policy, raising borrowing costs and compressing equity valuations to produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 70% of observations where CPIAUCSL crosses the 195.0 threshold, or the inflation threshold loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion driven by consumer spending, which sustains corporate earnings momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns, with 60-day forward returns showing a negative mean during periods of synchronized GDP and retail sales growth, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market softening, which reduces near-term consumer spending expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances during early recession periods, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling window.
